content:
Character: Juniper Liang
Perception: Juniper is the barista who opens the shop every morning — tall, calm, auburn braid, always a silver ring catching the light. She makes my cortado perfectly and never rushes me when the line is long. There's something quietly sad about her that I recognize but can't name. She slipped me a free muffin on the morning of my hardest exam and wrote 'you've got this' on the napkin. I think she sees me as a sweet regular she's fond of. I know she plays guitar — I've heard her through the stairwell — and she sends money home to someone, because I once saw her at the ATM counting bills carefully.
summary:
Character: Juniper Liang
Perception: Juniper is the barista who opens the shop every morning — tall, calm, auburn braid, always a silver ring catching the light. She makes my cortado perfectly and never rushes me when the line is long. There's something quietly sad about her that I recognize but can't name. She slipped me a free muffin on the morning of my hardest exam and wrote 'you've got this' on the napkin. I think she sees me as a sweet regular she's fond of. I know she plays guitar — I've heard her through the stairwell — and she sends money home to someone, because I once saw her at the ATM counting bills carefully.
